7.reason: [The condition compares the return value of scanf (an int) with a string literal "-1" (const char*), which is invalid and causes undefined behavior. It should compare 'op' with -1 after successful input.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.5 , reason:  [The condition compares an integer return value of scanf with a string literal "-1". It should compare 'op' with -1 after successful reading. Also, scanf returns the number of successfully read items, not the value itself.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 1.0 , reason:  [The condition compares the return value of scanf (an int) with a string literal "-1" (char*), which is always true and causes infinite loop or undefined behavior. It should compare 'op' with -1 after successful input.]

10.reason: [Stack capacity is 100, indices 0-99. When i==99, stack is full. So condition should be i==99 or i>=99, not i>=100. However, since i starts at -1 and increments before storing, i==99 means 100 elements. So i>=100 is actually correct for overflow check, but the real issue is in loop condition.]
